sglang.title: disable custom allreduce on HIP
sglang.author: hliuca
sglang.bodyExcerpt: The custom allreduce is enabled by default, and this hardware feature isn't supported now on AMD GPUs. So this PR disables nvlink on AMD GPUS by default, to reduce user options and to allow better user experience. Thank you.

sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

To fix microsoft/vscode-remote-release#49 (comment). The user in the container is "root" but outside container is whatever user you are. Any file changes made in the container are owned by root:root, causing permission issue.

sglang.title: Support loading of larger models with on-the-fly quantization
sglang.author: kwen2501
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Support loading of larger models.
Resolves request #2721.
Modifications
Quantizes a layer before loading another, thus keeping peak memory low.
The new load flow is represented by UI: --load-format layered.
Result
Test: apply --torchao-config int8wo to both Default loader and the proposed

sglang.title: Revert "disable custom allreduce on HIP"
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Reverts #3058
because AMD does support custom allreduce with its own vllm fork, which is used in the base image.

vllm.title: [VLM] Avoid unnecessary tokenization
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Instead of tokenizing the string mm_token * token_count, we can first tokenize mm_token by itself to mm_token_id before repeating it. This reduces the time to run the processing tests in test_common.py from 18 mins to 12 mins on my machine.

vllm.title: [Model][Bugfix]: correct Aria model output
vllm.author: xffxff
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
fix #12241
The output of python examples/offline_inference/vision_language.py -m aria in my local env is correct
> The image shows a beautiful scene with cherry blossoms in full bloom. The blossoms are in the foreground, framing the view. In the background, there is a tall tower, which appears to be

vllm.title: [Bugfix][VLM] Fix mixed-modality inference backward compatibility for V0
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a follow up of #12259.  When there are multiple modalities involved, embedding merge are based on different assumptions in V0 and V1, therefore get_input_embeddings does not work with V0 and will produce a bug when there are multiple modalities in a batch.
Fortunately, since embedding genera

vllm.title: [Misc] Fix the error in the tip for the --lora-modules parameter
vllm.author: WangErXiao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Since LoRAModulePath only contains three fields ('name', 'path', and 'base_model_name') and does not include a 'local_path' field, attempting to use 'local_path' caused an error as indicated by the usage tip. Therefore,  update the tip error message to reflect this.

vllm.title: [Misc]  Improve the readability of BNB error messages
vllm.author: jeejeelee

vllm.title: [Hardware][Gaudi][Bugfix] Fix HPU tensor parallelism, enable multiprocessing executor
vllm.author: kzawora-intel
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #11256 changed the input broadcasting flow a little bit, and in tensor parallel scenario we cannot assume that all workers will receive execute_model_req from the executor. Since broadcast from driver worker is now performed within LocalOrDistributedWorkerBase.prepare_input called by LocalOrDistribu

vllm.title: [Core] Support `reset_prefix_cache`
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds the capability of resetting prefix cache for both v0 and v1. This feature could be useful for the following cases:

In RLHF after weight syncing.
In benchmarking.

The prefix cache can only be reset when all blocks are free. However, although this PR also provide /reset_prefix_cache in

vllm.title: [Frontend][V1] Online serving performance improvements
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
These help in particular with TTFT, ITL variance, and overall throughput.

Break up output processing (detokenization) to avoid blocking the event loop for too long
Freeze the heap after startup to reduce GC overhead/pauses
Optimize a couple of CPU hotspots seen during profiling

Benchmark on A100:

vllm.title: [AMD][Quantization] Add TritonScaledMMLinearKernel since int8 is broken for AMD
vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We aren't able to run int8 models anymore, it's completely broken.  This because there is no TritonScaledMMLinearKernel class.
I added in TritonScaledMMLinearKernel. Since there is no Triton kernel to handle asymmetric int8 quantization, the new TritonScaledMMLinearKernel checks for this case and re

vllm.title: [Docs] Update FP8 KV Cache documentation
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Simplify the FP8 kv cache docs and include the recommended flow for calibrated scales

vllm.title: [Docs] Document vulnerability disclosure process
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Explicitly document the order of operations for how we will publish a
fix and advisory for a vulnerability.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [V1] Add `uncache_blocks`
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds .uncache_blocks to kv cache manager to uncache blocks if the num_computed_tokens  of the given request is reduced. This happens when speculative tokens were appended to verify but got rejected after reject sampling.
cc @LiuXiaoxuanPKU
